entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where agriculture and animal husbandry are interwoven.

entities.name: AGRICULTURE
entities.description: The sector involving crop production and land management.

entities.name: ANIMAL HUSBANDRY
entities.description: The branch of agriculture concerned with animals raised for labor or products.

entities.name: MIXED FARMING
entities.description: A system of farming that combines both crop production and livestock rearing.

entities.name: LIVESTOCK REARING
entities.description: The practice of raising livestock for agricultural purposes.

entities.name: RURAL LIVELIHOODS
entities.description: The means of securing basic necessities for people living in rural areas.

entities.name: INDIAN ECONOMY
entities.description: The economic system of India, influenced by the agriculture and livestock sectors.

entities.name: LIVESTOCK
entities.description: Domesticated animals raised in an agricultural setting.

entities.name: CROP RESIDUES
entities.description: The remains of crops after the harvest has been removed, used as fodder.

entities.name: GRAZING
entities.description: The act of livestock eating vegetation on common, private, or forest lands.

entities.name: FORAGE CROPS
entities.description: Plants grown specifically to be grazed by or fed to livestock.

entities.name: FODDER PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of producing food for livestock.

entities.name: GREEN FODDER
entities.description: Fresh, succulent forage crops used as livestock feed.

entities.name: NUTRIENTS
entities.description: Substances that provide nourishment essential for growth and maintenance of life.

entities.name: LIVESTOCK HEALTH
entities.description: The physical and physiological well-being of livestock.

entities.name: LIVESTOCK OUTPUT
entities.description: The productivity and yield derived from the livestock sector.

relationships.description: Interwoven in the context of Indian farming
Uses crop residues as a source of fodder
Uses grazing as a method to meet fodder needs
Uses forage crops as a source of fodder
Directly impacts the output of the livestock sector
